---
title: "Research"
toc: false
---

## Working papers

```{=html}
<div class="paper-card">
  <div class="paper-title">
    Weak instrumental variables due to nonlinearities in panel data:
    A Super Learner Control Function estimator
  </div>
  <div class="paper-meta">
    <span class="dot dot-wp"></span>
    Working paper &nbsp;·&nbsp; arXiv:2504.03228 &nbsp;·&nbsp; 2025
  </div>
  <details>
    <summary>Abstract</summary>
    <p class="abstract-text">
      I propose a control function estimator for panel data models with weak instruments arising
      from nonlinear first stages. The estimator uses a Super Learner ensemble to approximate the
      optimal first stage, then inserts estimated control functions into a partially linear second
      stage. I establish √NT-consistency, Neyman orthogonality with respect to first-stage
      nuisance, and inference validity under the generated regressors problem (Pagan 1984,
      Murphy & Topel 1985). Monte Carlo simulations across varied DGPs confirm the estimator's
      robustness to library misspecification and overfitting.
    </p>
  </details>
 <div class="paper-links">
    <a href="https://arxiv.org/abs/2504.03228" class="pill pill-arxiv">arXiv</a>
    <a href="assets/Slides_MonikaAvilaMarquez.pdf" class="pill">Slides</a>
    <a href="assets/EuroCim2026poster.pdf" class="pill">EuroCIM 2026 poster</a>
    <a href="mailto:monika.avila@unige.ch?subject=Request%3A%20SLCFE%20R%20package&body=Dear%20Monika%2C%0A%0AI%20am%20interested%20in%20accessing%20your%20SLCFE%20R%20package.%0A%0ABest%20regards%2C" class="pill">R package</a>
  </div>
</div>
```
```{=html}
<div class="paper-card">
  <div class="paper-title">
    Causal Identification under Interference: The Role of Treatment Assignment Independence
  </div>
  <div class="paper-coauthor">
  with <a href="https://www.juliusowusu.ca">Julius Owusu</a>
</div>
  <div class="paper-meta">
  <span class="dot dot-wp"></span> Working paper &nbsp;·&nbsp;
  <a href="https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.22532">arXiv:2604.22532</a> &nbsp;·&nbsp; 2026
  <div class="paper-status">Submitted</div>
</div>
  <details>
    <summary>Abstract</summary>
    <p class="abstract-text">
  Empirical researchers routinely invoke the no-interference or <em>individualistic treatment response</em> (ITR) assumption to identify causal effects in observational studies, despite concerns that interference across units may arise in many economic settings. This paper studies the causal content of standard ITR-based identification formulas when arbitrary interference is present. We show that, under restrictions on dependence between treatment assignments across units, conventional ITR-based identification formulas---including those underlying selection-on-observables, instrumental variables, regression discontinuity designs, and difference-in-differences---identify well-defined causal objects: types of <em>average direct effects</em> (ADEs). These results do not require knowledge of the interference structure or specification of exposure mappings. We also propose a sensitivity analysis framework that quantifies the robustness of statistical inference to violations of treatment-assignment independence under arbitrary interference.
</p>
  </details>
  <div class="paper-links">
    <a href="https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.22532" class="pill pill-arxiv">arXiv</a>
    <a href="#" class="pill">R package</a>
  </div>
</div>
```

## Work in progress

```{=html}
<div class="paper-card">
  <div class="paper-title">
   Model selection for crossed-random effects models for psychological experimental data
  </div>
  <div class="paper-coauthor">
  with <a href="https://www.unige.ch/fapse/mad/equipe/renaud">Olivier Renaud</a>
  <div class="paper-meta">
    <span class="dot dot-prog"></span> In progress &nbsp;·&nbsp; 2026
  </div>
  <div class="paper-links">
    <span class="pill">Draft available on request</span>
  </div>
</div>
```

## Publications
```{=html}
<div class="paper-card">
  <div class="paper-title">   
Dynamic Heterogeneous Linear Models for Three-level Panel Data with Short Time Dimension and Stratification
  </div>
  <div class="paper-coauthor">
  with <a href="">Jaya Krishnakumar</a>
  <div class="paper-meta">
    <span class="dot dot-prog"></span> Book Seven Decades of Econometrics and Beyond  &nbsp;·&nbsp; 2025
  </div>
  <div class="paper-links">
    <a href="https://link.springer.com/chapter/10.1007/978-3-031-92699-0_13" class="pill">Chapter link</a>
  </div>
</div>
</div>
```

```{=html}
<div class="paper-card">
  <div class="paper-title">   
Random Coefficients Models
  </div>
  <div class="paper-coauthor">
  with <a href="">Jaya Krishnakumar, and Laszlo Balaszi</a>
  <div class="paper-meta">
    <span class="dot dot-prog"></span> Book The Econometrics of Multi-dimensional Panels  &nbsp;·&nbsp; 2024
  </div>
  <div class="paper-links">
    <a href="https://link.springer.com/chapter/10.1007/978-3-031-49849-7_7" class="pill">Chapter link</a>
  </div>
</div>
```

## Presentations

| Year | Conference | Location |
|------|-----------|----------|
| 2026 | European Causal Inference Meeting (EuroCIM 2026) | University of Oxford |
